---
title: "Telegram"
platform_slug: "telegram"
sources:
  ugc:
    BR: data/global/telegram/ugc.yml
    EU: data/global/telegram/ugc.yml
    UK: data/regional/UK/telegram/ugc.yml
  ads:
    BR: data/global/telegram/ads.yml
    EU: data/global/telegram/ads.yml
    UK: data/global/telegram/ads.yml
---

Telegram's UGC transparency is assessed through the [Telegram API](https://core.telegram.org/api) across Brazil, the EU, and the UK; the platform provides no advertising transparency repository in any of the assessed regions. The advertising framework received a score of zero across all criteria, indicating a complete absence of any ad repository or programmatic access to advertising data. Under the UGC framework, the API enables free, structured extraction of public content data, supporting author-based search, keyword search, temporal filtering, historical data retrieval without time limits, and near-real-time content access. The most significant limitation is that the API does not permit membership-free, platform-wide content discovery: access is restricted to channels and groups in which the requesting account or bot is already a member. No graphical interface for data extraction exists, and documentation quality is uneven, lacking deprecation policies, per-endpoint rate-limit specifications, and practical usage examples. The UGC score places Telegram in the Restricted band, reflecting functional but structurally constrained API access; the absence of any advertising transparency infrastructure substantially limits research into sponsored content on the platform.

## Transparency Assessment


```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
import sys
from pathlib import Path
project_root = Path.cwd()
while not (project_root / 'utils').exists() and project_root != project_root.parent:
    project_root = project_root.parent
if str(project_root) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(project_root))
from utils.responses_aggregator import generate_platform_question_sections
```

```{python}
#| label: platform-summary
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
from utils.platform_summary import generate_platform_summary
generate_platform_summary("telegram")
```




## User-Generated Content

```{python}
#| label: ugc-questions
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
generate_platform_question_sections(
    platform="telegram",
    question_type="ugc",
    year="2025",
    heading_level=3,
)
```

## Advertising

```{python}
#| label: ads-questions
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
generate_platform_question_sections(
    platform="telegram",
    question_type="ads",
    year="2025",
    heading_level=3,
)
```
